back_translation: On the third day, Queen Esther put on her royal robes and went and stood in the inner court of the palace, opposite the throne hall. The king was sitting on his royal throne in the throne hall, facing the entrance.

When the king saw Queen Esther standing in the court, she found favor with him, and the king extended toward Esther the gold scepter that was in his hand. So Esther approached and touched the tip of the scepter.
The king said to her, 'What is it, Queen Esther? What is your request? Even up to half the kingdom, it will be granted to you.'
Queen Esther said, 'If it pleases the king, may the king and Haman come today to the banquet I have prepared for the king.'
The king said, 'Hurry and bring Haman, so we may do as Esther asks.' So the king and Haman went to the banquet that Esther had prepared.
While they drank wine, the king said to Esther, 'What is your petition? It will be granted. And what is your request? Even up to half the kingdom, it will be done.'
Queen Esther replied, 'My petition and my request are these:

If I have found favor in the sight of the king, and if it pleases the king to grant my petition and fulfill my request, may the king and Haman come to the banquet I will prepare for them both tomorrow. Then I will answer as the king has asked.'
That day Haman went out joyful and glad of heart. But when Haman saw Mordecai at the palace gate, who would not rise or tremble before him, Haman was filled with rage against Mordecai.
Nevertheless, Haman restrained himself and went home. He sent for and invited his friends and Zeresh his wife.
Haman recounted to them his glorious wealth, his many sons, and all the ways the king had promoted and exalted him above the officials and servants of the king.
Haman went on, 'What is more, Queen Esther invited no one but me to join the king at the banquet she prepared, and tomorrow I am again invited by her along with the king.
Yet all this does not satisfy me at all, as long as I see Mordecai the Jew sitting at the palace gate.'

Then Zeresh his wife and all his friends said to him, 'Have a gallows fifty cubits high built, and in the morning ask the king to have Mordecai hanged on it. Then go to the banquet with the king and enjoy yourself.' The advice pleased Haman, and he had the gallows built.
